candidates.why: A separate operating D&A line exists above financial items, but it is generic and not clearly tied to cost of sales / cost of revenues relative to the selected COGS bucket 'Materials and services'. Using it for COGS_DA risks misallocating group-wide depreciation.

candidates.row_label: Amortization excluding Goodwill in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: Explicitly tied to R&D, not to COGS. It should not be added to the COGS bucket.
Looks like a residual balancing component within the D&A note structure, not a clear standalone COGS-related D&A row.

variables.notes: No row in the operating section explicitly identifies depreciation/amortisation as belonging to COGS / cost of sales. The visible D&A rows are generic or R&D-specific, so the conservative no-double-count choice is to add nothing separately to COGS.

candidates.why: This is the clearest separate operating D&A line above financial items and sits alongside the main operating expense buckets rather than beneath 'Personnel expenses' or 'Other operating expenses'. If any D&A were to be added separately to the selected XSGA bucket, this summary line is the best candidate.

candidates.row_label: Amortization excluding Goodwill in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: Explicit R&D-related amortisation line, but the excerpt already contains a separate above-operating-profit R&D row ('Other R&D Expenses') and prior mapping already handles R&D through both XSGA and XRD logic. Adding this separately would risk distortion/double counting under the stated R&D interaction rule.
Appears to be a tiny balancing/residual line within the D&A breakdown, not the preferred summary row.

variables.notes: R:c. There is a separate operating-level summary row 'Depreciation', suggesting D&A is not embedded in the selected XSGA rows. However, that row is generic and may include depreciation beyond SG&A, while the only clearly attributed component is R&D amortisation, which should not be added separately here because R&D is already handled via the XSGA/XRD setup. Manual review is warranted if functional allocation of the generic depreciation line is available.
